items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Old Scatness
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* highlands
*County:* Shetland
*Type:* castle
*Period:* Bronze Age to Viking
*Coords:* 59.879, -1.3054
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Old_Scatness
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Old%20Scatness%20Shetland%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/62/Old_Scatness_Dig_-_geograph.org.uk_-_355975.jpg/330px-Old_Scatness_Dig_-_geograph.org.uk_-_355975.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Old Scatness is an archeological site on Scat Ness, near the village of Scatness, in the parish of Dunrossness in the south end of Mainland, Shetland, Scotland, near Sumburgh Airport. It consists of medieval, Viking, Pictish, and Iron Age remains and has been a settlement for thousands of years, each new generation adding buildings, and levelling off old ones. Among the discoveries is an Iron Age broch, the Ness of Burgi fort.

_Listing 1 of 10_

listing.name: Old Scatness
listing.desc: Old Scatness is an archeological site on Scat Ness, near the village of Scatness, in the parish of Dunrossness in the south end of Mainland, Shetland, Scotland, near Sumburgh Airport. It consists of medieval, Viking, Pictish, and Iron Age remains and has been a settlement for thousands of years, each new generation adding buildings, and levelling off old ones. Among the discoveries is an Iron Age broch, the Ness of Burgi fort.

🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Varrich Castle
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* highlands
*County:* Highland
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 14th century
*Coords:* 58.47575556, -4.43543889
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Castle_Varrich
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Varrich%20Castle%20Highland%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/af/2011_Schotland_Tongue_Varrich_Castle_op_morene_met_Ben_Hope_3-06-2011_19-27-33.png/330px-2011_Schotland_Tongue_Varrich_Castle_op_morene_met_Ben_Hope_3-06-2011_19-27-33.png
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Castle Varrich is a castle in the far north of the Scottish Highlands, near the village of Tongue. The castle is on a local high point of rock, overlooking both the Kyle of Tongue and the village of Tongue. The castle's precise origins and age are unknown.

_Listing 2 of 10_

listing.name: Varrich Castle
listing.desc: Castle Varrich is a castle in the far north of the Scottish Highlands, near the village of Tongue. The castle is on a local high point of rock, overlooking both the Kyle of Tongue and the village of Tongue. The castle's precise origins and age are unknown.

🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Ardtornish Castle
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* highlands
*County:* Highland
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 13th century
*Coords:* 56.51916667, -5.75388889
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ardtornish_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Ardtornish%20Castle%20Highland%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZNuH3dN7otO3r-qcIiCDnGZB9z0ElNx37UmKkjJMXzoqV15RbOjMAEgoOHMIintklxRmCqywokYA7AfXebsyvtZ6iH4SR1JwNtlAaD5o9nf6WetGxg3HF6ASZW0ld0-5i8gpNfUd8DkDVPPsQ=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown

*Description:*
Ardtornish Castle is a castle situated in the grounds of the Ardtornish estate in Morvern, on the west coast of Scotland. It stands at the seaward end of a promontory which extends in a southerly direction into the Sound of Mull, about 1 mile south-east of the village of Lochaline, Highland. It is protected as a scheduled monument.

_Listing 3 of 10_

It is protected as a scheduled monument.

*Description:*
Dalcross Castle is a restored 17th century tower house, about 1+1⁄2 miles southwest of Croy, Highland, Scotland, and about 7 miles northeast of Inverness. The castle stands on a ridge.

items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Cromarty Courthouse
*Country:* scotland
*Region:* highlands
*County:* Highland
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 18th century
*Coords:* 57.68, -4.0312
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cromarty_Courthouse
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Cromarty%20Courthouse%20Highland%20scotland
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ad/The_old_court_house_%28geograph_6600836%29.jpg/330px-The_old_court_house_%28geograph_6600836%29.jpg
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* wikipedia
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* add source https://www.cromartymuseum.org.uk/

*Description:*
Cromarty Courthouse, formerly Cromarty Town House, is a municipal building in Church Street, Cromarty, in the Highland area of Scotland. The structure, which is used as a museum, is a Category A listed building.

_Listing 6 of 10_

listing.name: Cromarty Courthouse
listing.desc: Cromarty Courthouse, formerly Cromarty Town House, is a municipal building in Church Street, Cromarty, in the Highland area of Scotland. The structure, which is used as a museum, is a Category A listed building.
